questions.question:
Correct the following code: function App() { const abc='Hello'; return (

Friends {2+2}

); } export default App;
questions.answer: {} missing at: <Example fname={abc} age='30'/>
questions.explanation: In JSX, JavaScript variables must be wrapped in curly braces {}. fname=abc is invalid; it should be fname={abc} to pass the variable value.

questions.question: What output will be generated by following program using Example.js with Props.Name and Prop.Price (note: Prop not Props)?
questions.answer: It will give an error regarding prop
questions.explanation: The code uses 'Prop.Price' (without 's') which is undefined. 'Props' is the parameter but 'Prop' is not defined, causing a ReferenceError.
options: It will give an error regarding prop

questions.question: Which command is used to create a React app using Vite?
questions.answer: npm create vite@latest myApp
questions.explanation: Vite uses 'npm create vite@latest appName' to scaffold a new project. The older 'npx create-react-app' uses Webpack, not Vite.

questions.question: One key advantage of Vite over traditional bundlers is:
questions.answer: Faster development server startup
questions.explanation: Vite uses native ES modules in the browser and only bundles on-demand, making dev server startup nearly instant compared to Webpack-based tools.

questions.question: What is Vite mainly used for?
questions.answer: Frontend build tool and development server
questions.explanation: Vite is a modern frontend build tool that provides a fast development server and optimized production builds. It supports React, Vue, Svelte, etc.
options: Frontend build tool and development server

questions.question: What will be the output of following program? button has onClick={handleclick()} — note the parentheses calling the function.
questions.answer: It will show an alert box once page will be loaded for first time, and it will perform nothing while clicking on button
questions.explanation: onClick={handleclick()} immediately invokes the function during rendering (not on click). The result (undefined) is assigned to onClick, so clicking the button does nothing.
options: By clicking on click me button it will show an alert box
It will show an alert box once page will be loaded for first time, and it will perform nothing while clicking on button

questions.question: What will be the correct syntax to perform any function on double click in REACT?
questions.answer: <button onDoubleClick={doubleClickHandler}>Double Click here</button>
questions.explanation: In React, the double-click event is 'onDoubleClick' (camelCase). The handler is passed as a reference {handler}, NOT called {handler()}.

questions.question: Which of the following port is the default where React server runs?
questions.explanation: Create React App starts the development server on port 3000 by default. Vite uses port 5173 by default.

questions.question: What are arbitrary inputs of components in React also known as?
questions.answer: Props
questions.explanation: Props (Properties) are inputs passed to React components from parent components. They are read-only and flow down the component tree.

questions.question: Select correct syntax to render output with 'LJU' heading and textfield.
questions.answer:
<>

LJU
questions.explanation: React components must return a single root element. Using a Fragment <></> wraps multiple elements without adding extra DOM nodes.

questions.question: What will be the output of the following program? const x = 9; let text = 'Welcome'; x = 10; ...
questions.answer: Error will be generated
questions.explanation: 'const x = 9' declares x as a constant. x = 10 attempts to reassign it, which throws a TypeError: Assignment to constant variable.

questions.question: Correct syntax to write Arrow function: Msg = ____ 'Hi, there!';
questions.explanation: Arrow function syntax is: const Msg = () => 'Hi, there!'; The fat arrow '=>' is the defining feature of arrow functions in ES6.

questions.question:
Identify error/output in following code: const arr=[10,20,30,40,50]; let sum=0; return(
{ arr.map((v) => { sum=sum+v; return

sum = {sum}

}) }
);
questions.answer: Sum is printed correctly for each iteration in <h1> tag
questions.explanation: This is valid JSX. sum accumulates: 10, 30, 60, 100, 150 and each is printed in an h1 tag. No error occurs as sum=sum+v is valid JS inside the map callback.
options: error, since sum=sum+v statement must be specified using JSX
Sum is printed correctly for each iteration in <h1> tag
error, since let sum=0 statement is specified at wrong place
sum is printed as 'NaN' for each iteration in <h1> tag

questions.question: Which is the correct syntax to get output of expression between HTML tags?
questions.answer:
const a=1; const b=1; return(

{a+b}

)
questions.explanation: In JSX, JavaScript expressions are written inside single curly braces {}. Double braces {{}} are for inline style objects, not expressions.

questions.question: What will be the output of the following program where parent passes Info = { name: 'ABC' } as a prop named 'name', and child accesses props.name.name?
questions.answer:
What is your name?
My name is ABC!
questions.explanation: Info is an object {name:'ABC'}. It is passed as name={Info}. So props.name = {name:'ABC'}, and props.name.name = 'ABC'. Both lines render correctly.
options:
What is your name?
My name is ABC!

questions.question: What will be the output of below program importing 'contact' (lowercase) from './Contact' and using <contact> in JSX?
questions.answer: SyntaxError
questions.explanation: React treats JSX elements starting with a lowercase letter as HTML elements. <contact> is treated as an unknown HTML tag, not the component. Also BrowserRouter usage outside App would cause issues. The component must be imported with uppercase: import Contact.
options: It will give an error as <BrowserRouter> tag is used in place of <Router> tag.
It will not throw an error but won't display anything.

questions.question: How many numbers of elements a valid React component can return?
questions.explanation: A React component must return exactly one root element. To return multiple, wrap them in a <div> or React Fragment (<> </>).

questions.question: What is the declarative way to render a dynamic list of components based on values in an array?
questions.answer: Using the Array.map() method
questions.explanation: Array.map() transforms each item into a JSX element. It's the standard React pattern for rendering dynamic lists.

questions.question: In which of the following directory React.js components can be saved?
questions.answer: Inside the src folder
questions.explanation: React components are typically placed in the src/ folder. The public/ folder is for static assets; node_modules/ is for packages.

questions.question: What will be the output of the following code? <Example fname='A' + ' B' + ' C'/> — string concatenation with + in JSX attribute
questions.answer: Error will be generated
questions.explanation: In JSX, you cannot use + operator directly inside a string prop like fname='A'+'B'. The expression would need to be in curly braces: fname={'A'+'B'+'C'}. This causes a syntax error.

questions.question: What will be the output when style has backgroundcolor (lowercase c) instead of backgroundColor?
questions.answer:
LJU
age
questions.explanation: In React, CSS properties must be in camelCase. 'backgroundcolor' is not recognized, so the background color won't apply, but no error is thrown — 'age' is printed as text (not 30, since it's hardcoded string in JSX as the text 'age').
options:
LJU
Display 'age' with blue background color

questions.question: How to write comments in JSX?
questions.explanation: JSX comments use {/* comment here */} syntax. Regular HTML <!-- --> comments don't work inside JSX.

questions.question: What will be the output of code that maps over an array of 2 image objects and renders each with an img tag?
questions.answer: 2 Images will be displayed vertically on browser.
questions.explanation: Both images are in separate <div> elements stacked vertically. The map correctly iterates over both items so both images render. Default block layout = vertical.
options: It will display 2 images side by side on browser
It will only display first image on browser
2 Images will be displayed vertically on browser.
It will only display last image on browser.

questions.question: Consider code with two sibling <div> elements returned from App without a Fragment wrapper. Identify error/output.
questions.answer: Error as JSX fragment tag must required
questions.explanation: React components cannot return multiple root-level elements without wrapping them. A Fragment <> </> or a <div> wrapper is required.
options:
This is Demo1
This is Demo2

This is Demo1
This id Demo2
Error as JSX fragment tag must required

questions.question: Consider code where obj JSON is passed as prop 'name' to Example component and props.name.type is accessed. Identify error/output.
questions.answer: 'car' is printed successfully by referring props.name.type
questions.explanation: A JSON object can be passed as a prop. props.name = obj = {type:'car', models:[...]}. So props.name.type = 'car'. No error occurs.
options: 'car' is printed successfully by referring props.name.type
Error as props.name.type is invalid use of props to refer type
Error since type must be fetched by passing 'string' rather than 'obj'
Error as JSON object can not be passed to other component.

questions.question: What will following code do? arr.filter that returns false for uppercase values and true for lowercase?
questions.answer: Returns array having only small letters
questions.explanation: If value === value.toUpperCase() (uppercase), return false (exclude). Otherwise return true (include). So lowercase letters pass the filter.
options: Returns array having only capital letters
Returns array having only small letters
Error as not proper use of 'toUpperCase()'

questions.question:
Fill the blanks in code to map over images array and render each image: {______.map((val) => { return (
) })}
questions.answer: images,src={val.pic}
questions.explanation: The array is named 'images' so images.map() is correct. Each object has a 'pic' property so src={val.pic} is the correct JSX expression.

questions.question: What will be the output of code that maps over ['a','java','Full Stack','567','Python'] and calls toUpperCase() on each?
questions.answer:
map function
array values= A
array values= JAVA
array values= FULL STACK
array values= 567
array values= PYTHON
questions.explanation: toUpperCase() works on strings. '567' is already a string element so '567'.toUpperCase() = '567'. All elements render correctly.
options:
map function
array values= A
array values= JAVA
array values= FULL STACK
array values= 567
array values= PYTHON
Error:567 can't be added in list
Error:all inputs should be of type in array

questions.question: Identify correct statement to apply CSS object 'css' on a paragraph in JSX.
questions.answer: This is my data
questions.explanation: When CSS is a pre-defined JavaScript object, use style={css}. Double braces {{}} are needed only when writing the object inline (style={{color:'red'}}).

questions.question: Identify error/output: arr=[10,2,30,3,50] filtered to exclude multiples of 10, then joined with comma.
questions.explanation: v%10===0 returns false for 10, 30, 50 (excluded). 2 and 3 return true (included). newarr=[2,3]. join(',') = '2,3'.

questions.question: Identify correct code to change CSS, attributes and text of a paragraph on double click using e.target.
questions.answer: using e.target.innerHTML with fontFamily
questions.explanation: e.target.innerHTML (not .value) changes text content of non-input elements. fontFamily (camelCase) is correct. font-family (hyphenated) would be invalid in JS style object.

questions.question: What will be the output of below program? import {Router,Route,Routes,Link} from 'react'; ...
questions.answer: Cause of error is Router/Route/Routes/Link is imported from react
questions.explanation: React Router components (BrowserRouter, Route, Routes, Link) must be imported from 'react-router-dom', NOT from 'react'. Importing from 'react' will cause an error.
options: It will give an error as <BrowserRouter> tag is not used
Reached at the end - no executable code
Cause of error is Router/Route/Routes/Link is imported from react

questions.question: In ReactJS, to filter an array of objects named 'dataArr' based on item.id === 1, which option shows correct syntax?
questions.answer: dataArr.filter(item => item.id === 1)
questions.explanation: Array.filter() takes a callback that returns true/false. Using === for strict equality comparison on id is the correct functional approach in React.

questions.question: In a React application, ParentComponent passes prop 'username' to ChildComponent. Which option correctly accesses and displays the username prop in ChildComponent?
questions.answer: {props.username}
questions.explanation: In functional components, props is the parameter. Props are accessed as props.propName. In class components you'd use this.props.propName.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the correct syntax for rendering a React component named 'Button' with JSX?
questions.answer: <Button></Button>
questions.explanation: React components in JSX start with an uppercase letter and use angle bracket syntax. <Button></Button> or self-closing <Button /> are both correct.

questions.question:
Complete the array method that will allow you to run a function on each item in the array and return a new array: const myList = myArray.________((item) =>

{item}

)
questions.answer: map
questions.explanation: Array.map() creates a new array by running a function on each element. It's the standard method for transforming arrays in React rendering.

questions.question: What is the correct syntax to write an expression in JSX?
questions.answer: { expression }
questions.explanation: JSX expressions are enclosed in single curly braces { }. Double braces are only for inline objects like style={{color:'red'}}.

questions.question: Which of the following options correctly represents JSX syntax in a React functional component?
questions.answer: Hello, World!
questions.explanation:
JSX looks like HTML inside JavaScript.
Hello, World!
is valid JSX that renders a div element with text content.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to access a function display() from h1 element in JSX?
questions.answer: {display()}
questions.explanation: To call a function and display its return value in JSX, use {display()} inside the element — single braces for the expression and () to invoke the function.

questions.question: Which event handler in React is used to handle a form submission in a functional component?
questions.answer: onSubmit
questions.explanation: onSubmit is the React event handler for form submission. It maps to the native DOM 'submit' event. Used as <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>.

questions.question: Identify error/output: arr=['apple','banana','kiwi','orange'] filtered to keep fruits with length > 5, then joined with comma.
questions.answer: Output:banana,orange
questions.explanation: apple=5 letters (not > 5), banana=6 ✓, kiwi=4, orange=6 ✓. Filter returns ['banana','orange']. join(',') = 'banana,orange'.
options: Error: The filter function should have a closing parenthesis.
Error: JSX elements should be returned within a single parent element.

questions.question: Which syntax is commonly used for defining a route in React using react-router-dom?
questions.answer: <Route path='/about' element={<About />} />
questions.explanation: React Router v6 uses the element prop with a JSX element: element={<Component />}. The older v5 syntax used component={Component}.

questions.question: Give the correct output for: name='John', age=30. Renders h1 Hello John, p You are 30 years old, ul with ternary (age>18 ? adult : minor).
questions.answer: Hello, John! and 'You are 30 years old.' with an unordered list item 'You are an adult'
questions.explanation: age=30 > 18 is true so 'You are an adult' is shown. All JSX is syntactically correct. The ternary operator works correctly in JSX.
options: Hello, John! and 'You are 30 years old.' with an unordered list item 'You are an adult'
Hello, John! and 'You are 30 years old.' with an unordered list item 'You are a minor'
An error will occur due to incorrect syntax in the JSX
Nothing will be displayed on the screen

questions.question: Consider code where obj1={Name:'Hiyan',Age:32,Branch:['CSE','CE','IT']} is passed as prop Name, and child accesses props.Name.Branch[0]. Identify error/output.
questions.answer: CSE will be printed on browser in <h1> tag
questions.explanation: props.Name = obj1 = {Name:'Hiyan',Age:32,Branch:['CSE','CE','IT']}. So props.Name.Branch[0] = 'CSE'. This is valid and renders correctly.
options: CSE will be printed on browser in <h1> tag
Prints entire JSON object including all members
CE will be printed on browser in <h1> tag

questions.question:
Identify error/output: const a='Hello'; const b=8; return(

The addition is: {a+2+b}

);
questions.answer: Output:'The addition is: Hello28' with green background color in <h1> tag
questions.explanation: a+'Hello', then 'Hello'+2 = 'Hello2' (string concatenation), then 'Hello2'+8 = 'Hello28'. JavaScript evaluates left to right with implicit string conversion.
options: Output:'The addition is: Hello10' with green background color in <h1> tag.
Output:'The addition is: Hello28' with green background color in <h1> tag
Output:'The addition is: 28Hello' with green background color in <h1> tag

questions.question: What will be the output of code that sorts arr=[2,4,6,3,7,5] then maps each value adding 3 and concatenating '2'?
questions.explanation: arr.sort() sorts lexicographically: [2,3,4,5,6,7]. Then val+3+'2': 2+3=5 → '5'+'2'='52'; 3+3=6 → '62'; etc. Because '2' is a string, concatenation occurs.

questions.question: Random numbers stored as array 'arr'. How can you use the filter method to get only even numbers?
questions.answer: arr.filter(num => num % 2 === 0)
questions.explanation: num % 2 === 0 returns true for even numbers (remainder of dividing by 2 is 0). filter keeps items where the callback returns true.

questions.question: Identify correct CSS syntax in code for mystyle to get proper output when used as style={mystyle}.
questions.answer: {fontSize:'40px', color:'green', textTransform:'uppercase'}
questions.explanation: React inline styles require camelCase property names (fontSize not font-size). The style object is a JS object with string values.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an incorrect statement regarding React? 1.React components composed with only webpack. 2.All components must act like recursive functions. 3.map() is a callback called for each element. 4.First file loaded is index.js. 5.Virtual DOM handles async operations.
questions.answer: Only 3
questions.explanation: Statement 3 is correct: .map() is a callback called for each element. Statements 1,2,4,5 are incorrect (React works without webpack; pure functions not recursive; index.html loads first; virtual DOM is not for async).

questions.question:
What do you call the message wrapped in curly braces below? let message = 'Welcome to LJU'; const element =

{message}

;
questions.answer: a JS expression
questions.explanation: Curly braces in JSX contain JavaScript expressions. {message} evaluates the variable message and embeds its value into the JSX.

questions.question: What will be the output of the following JSX code? <button onClick={() => alert('Button clicked!')}>Click me</button>
questions.answer: A button with the text 'Click me' that triggers an alert when clicked.
questions.explanation: The arrow function () => alert('Button clicked!') is passed as a reference to onClick. Clicking the button executes it, showing the alert.
options: A warning about the missing onClick handler.
A syntax error due to the arrow function syntax.
A button with the text 'Click me' that triggers an alert when clicked.

questions.question: What will be the output of: arr=[10,20,30,40,50] mapped with index*5?
questions.explanation: map receives value and index. Index starts at 0. s = index*5: 0*5=0, 1*5=5, 2*5=10, 3*5=15, 4*5=20. The array values are not used.

questions.question: In a React form, which event is typically used to handle changes in an input field?
questions.answer: onChange
questions.explanation: onChange fires every time the input value changes. It's used for controlled components in React where the state tracks the input value in real time.

questions.question: Build basic react app that display 'Hello World' in browser.
questions.explanation: A React functional component returns JSX with an h1 tag. The component is exported and mounted in main.jsx using ReactDOM.createRoot().

questions.question: Create a ReactJS app which shows how to pass and read data using props.
questions.explanation: Props are passed from App to Greeting like HTML attributes. In the child component, they are accessed via the props object parameter.

questions.question: Write a React code to print car's brand name and its model name which are passed as props using JSON.
questions.explanation: A JSON object is passed as a single prop 'car'. The child component accesses individual properties using props.car.brand, props.car.model, etc.

questions.question: Write react js script to display alert box with text 'Welcome to LJU' by clicking on button.
questions.explanation: handleClick is defined as an arrow function. onClick={handleClick} passes the function reference (not invoked). Clicking the button calls the function which shows the alert.

questions.question: Write react js script to display values in console while changing it in text box.
questions.explanation: useState manages the input value. onChange fires on every keystroke. e.target.value gives the current input. console.log prints each change to the browser console.

questions.question: Write react js script to display alert box with text 'welcome to lju' only on double click button
questions.explanation: Use the onDoubleClick event handler in JSX (camelCase). Pass the function reference without parentheses to avoid calling it immediately on render. The alert fires only when the user double-clicks the button.

questions.question:
Create react app to perform tasks as asked.
1. Home.js - for the home page content
2. Shop.js - for the shop page content
3. Contact.js - for the contact page content
4. Nopage.js - for the page other than mentioned links
Create Main.js file which contains Links for Home, Shop and Product page. Also, add functionality of page routing.
Finally call Main.js in App.js.
questions.explanation: React Router DOM provides BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, and Link. BrowserRouter wraps everything; Routes contains Route definitions; Link creates navigation links without page reload; path='*' catches all unmatched routes (404).

questions.question: Create React app to pass student name, roll number, t1 marks and t2 marks of 2 students to component and read the information and display in table format. Use Props.
questions.explanation: Props are passed as attributes on the component tag. The child component receives them via the props object and renders each in a <tr>/<td> inside a table. App.js renders two StudentCard instances with different prop values.

questions.question:
Create react app and use routing functionality of react to perform the tasks as asked.
Create one file named Main.js which contains links for home and about page and routing functionality.
In Home.js file add one image and heading(h1) 'LJ University'
In About.js file add branch list (CSE,IT,CE).
questions.explanation: Images in React are imported like modules using 'import logo from ./logo.png'. BrowserRouter handles navigation; Link components prevent full-page refresh. Each Route maps a path to a component.

questions.question: Write a ReactJS code in which we have apply filter to skip digits which are less than '10' from the array and display all remaining digits of the array [3,5,11,4,17,8,21,2,26,13,8]. Pass values as props.
questions.explanation:
The array is passed as a prop named 'numbers'. Inside FilterComp, filter() keeps only values >= 10 (i.e., skips values less than 10). map() renders each remaining number in a

tag. key prop is needed for list rendering.

questions.question: Create React app to pass color(red), background color(yellow), font size(25px) and font style(italic) as properties to component and apply css to 'Lj Students' text written in p tag. Use Props
questions.explanation: CSS properties in JSX use camelCase (backgroundColor, fontSize, fontStyle). The style object is built from props received by the child component. Props are passed as attributes from the parent (App.js).

questions.question:
Write react js script to perform the tasks as asked below.
Add an array of objects which contains name and age of people.
Apply filter to find people with age less than 40. And display name of all people using map function. Use props.
questions.explanation: An array of objects is passed as a prop. The child uses filter() to get people with age < 40, then map() to render only their names. Always add a unique key prop when rendering lists.

questions.question:
Write react js script to perform the tasks as asked below.
Add an array of objects which contains name and branch(CSE,IT,CE) of students.
Apply filter to find students who are from CSE branch. And display name of all students of CSE branch using map function.
questions.explanation: The students array is passed as props to StudentList. filter() checks if branch === 'CSE'. map() renders only the name of filtered students. The parent component owns the data; the child just renders it.

questions.question:
Create react app and use routing functionality of react to perform the tasks as asked.
Create one file named Main.js which contains links for React Js, Node Js and Express Js and routing functionality.
Create components for each links and add heading 'React JS','Node JS' and 'Express JS' in component files Ex1.js, Ex2.js and EX3.js respectively.
questions.explanation: Three separate component files (Ex1, Ex2, Ex3) are imported in Main.js. Routes maps each URL path to its respective component. Link provides SPA navigation without reloading the page.

questions.question:
Create a React app to perform tasks as asked using functional component:
1) Create a React Router that includes three routes: Home, Food Items, and Contact and implement navigation between these routes.
2) Create a route that displays a Home page, Food Items details page and Contact details page.
3) When a user clicks on Home page it should navigate to the home page and display 'Welcome to LJU' in bold in the h1 heading. When a user clicks on a Food Items page, it should navigate to the Food Items Detail page and display three products' information with name, price and description using props. And when the user clicks on Contact page it should navigate to contact details page and display contact information with blue color font.
questions.explanation: FoodCard is a reusable component that receives name, price, description as props. FoodItems renders three FoodCards. Contact uses inline style with color:'blue'. BrowserRouter+Routes+Route handles navigation.

questions.question: Create react app to pass product image, name and price as properties from one component to another component. Add an array of objects with pic, name and price properties of 2 products. Display Image name and price of the products in browser using map method.
questions.explanation: Images are imported and stored in an array of objects. map() iterates over the array and renders a ProductCard for each product by spreading props. display:'flex' places cards side by side.

questions.question: Create react app using function component to pass background color(green), font size(50px) font style(italic),text transform(uppercase) as properties to Example.js component and apply css to 'This is an React Application' text written in h1 tag in Example2.js component. Use props.
questions.explanation: Props flow from App -> Example -> Example2. CSS properties are camelCased in JSX (backgroundColor, fontStyle, textTransform). Example acts as a pass-through/relay component forwarding props to Example2.

questions.question:
Create react app using function component to perform tasks as asked.
First create files as asked below in folder named 'component'
1. Login.js - for the login page content
2. Product.js - for the Product page content
3. Contact.js - for the contact page content
4. About.js - for the about page content
Create Home.js file which contains Links for Login, Product, Contact and About page. Also, add functionality of page routing.
Finally call Home.js in App.js.
questions.explanation: Components are placed in a 'component' subfolder. Imports use relative paths './component/...'. Home.js is the routing hub — it imports all page components, defines Links and Routes, and is called from App.js.

questions.question: Write a react.js file to pass an integer value to a component, which is defined as a property in app.js file. Fetch that value in component and print its table on console. Write all necessary files i.e. App.js and Component.js. Perform task by function component.
questions.explanation: The integer 7 is passed as 'number' prop. useEffect runs after render and logs the multiplication table in the console. The for loop iterates i from 1 to 10 and prints num * i. Change 7 to any integer in App.js.

questions.question: Write React JS script to display three products. Products having title, product image, price and description. All three products should be horizontally adjacent to each other. Note: using props show all products and use Functional component for complete this script.
questions.explanation: flexDirection:'row' on the parent div places the three ProductCard components horizontally. Each card receives title, image, price, and description as props and renders them inside a styled div.

questions.question:
Write react js script to perform the tasks as asked below using functional component:
1) Add an array of objects which contains name and age of people.
2) Apply filter to find people with age less than 40. And display name of all people using map function. Use props.
questions.explanation: Array is passed as prop 'people'. filter() returns only objects with age < 40. map() renders the name of each filtered person. key prop prevents React warnings for list items.

questions.question:
Create a React app to perform tasks as asked using functional component:
1) Create a react Router that includes three routes: Home, Product, and Contact and implement navigation between these routes.
2) Create a route that displays a Home page, Product details page and Contact details page.
3) When a user clicks on Home page it should navigate to home page and display 'Welcome to LJU' in bold in h1 heading. When a user clicks on a Product page, it should navigate to the product detail page and display three products information with price and description using props. And when user clicks on Contact page it should navigate to contact details page and display contact information with blue color font.
questions.explanation: Three-route React app. ProductCard is reused three times with different props inside Product.js. Contact applies blue color via inline style. BrowserRouter enables client-side routing without page reload.

questions.question: Write a program to create function based ReactJS app having an array of 6 people having mentioned age [70,83,38,65,49,94] and display list of people whose age is greater than 60 using filter method. Also display total count of people whose age is greater than 60 on the same page. Use props.
questions.explanation: Ages from the array are embedded in an array of objects with names. filter() returns people with age > 60 (Person1:70, Person2:83, Person4:65, Person6:94). seniors.length gives the count which is displayed below the list.

questions.question: Create a React function component named File1 that receives two props, productName and price. The component should display the product name and price. Additionally, add a button labeled 'Add to cart' to the component. When the button is clicked onClick event triggered and an alert should be displayed with the message 'Your Product is Added to cart!'.
questions.explanation: File1 receives productName and price as props and renders them. handleClick is a function that calls alert(). The button's onClick={handleClick} (without parentheses) registers the function as a handler — called only when clicked.

questions.question:
Write react js script to perform the task as below using function component:
- Create array of objects which have id, name & city of person.
- Display id & name of persons having city name 'Ahmedabad'. Use props.
No need to write App.js file.
questions.explanation: PersonList receives 'persons' as a prop. filter() checks city === 'Ahmedabad'. map() renders each filtered person's id and name. The unique id is used as the key prop for efficient React rendering.
